channel.display-name: The Price is Right: The Barker Era

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey takes a Polaris Ranger and decks it out; after that, he gives a sneak peek at the new Ironworker.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey puts a programmer on the Drag 'N' Wagon to finally bring the stripped down Ram Hot Rod truck to life; he lays out how to set up a shop with custom flooring, custom cabinets and several tools and toolboxes.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey brings the International Cabover truck back into the shop to be outfitted with a vintage Holmes dual wrecker bed and the big truck continues to come together using parts from a junkyard, a swap meet, and even some new after market parts.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Converting a Long Bed Truck Into a Short Bed
programme.desc: Stacey takes a classic 1967 Chevy long bed, and shows how to cut the frame properly, so you have a safe and reliable truck when you are done.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.sub-title: History of NASA and the Moon Buggy Race
programme.desc: Stacey takes a look at the amazing roots of the Space Program, and the incredible invention of the moon buggy. Then it's out to the NASA testing grounds to see the annual Moon buggy race.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Putting a New Short Bed on a Classic Truck
programme.desc: Stacey picks up right where he left off a few weeks ago, and proceeds to install a brand new short bed on the chassis of a truck that was born a LONG BED.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: The P-38 Lighting - WWII Legend & Jon Kaase Small Block Ford Upgrades
programme.desc: A look at what was one of the most lethal airplanes of WWII, the P-38 Lightning; Stacey takes a look at what made it so unique and why the German army nicknamed it the "forked tailed devil".

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Lawn and garden tools are often neglected as mowers, weed-eaters, chain saws and more such things get thrown in a garage in the fall, never to be touched again until spring; Stacey walks through the simple steps on how to care for these tools.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: The V8 Interceptor is back and wearing a striking coat of glossy black paint. Stacey walks you through the process and shows off the incredible paint and body work that was done by the Hot Rod Institute.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Heavy Metal Tow Truck and Edwards Manufacturing
programme.desc: Stacey rolls vintage tow truck Heavy Metal back into the shop to outfit it with new electric winches, an amazing custom tool box, and a light bar.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: The Ray Barton Story and the V8 Interceptor Interior
programme.desc: Ray Barton Racing Engines.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Sgt. Rock Exhaust and Heavy Metal Seats
programme.desc: Sgt. Rock takes care of steering, brakes and the exhaust system.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: The wild twin-turbo system.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Super Cars, Hot Rods and Go-Karts
programme.desc: Stacey rolls the SL-C back into the shop to show off the wild twin turbo system that Schwartz Performance put together for it, and he tackles the problem areas with building a vehicle and how to pick the right project.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey and the Crew build the new GEARZ shop; tips and tricks for setting up a useable work space.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey makes a house call to Pennsylvania and shows you how to take a vehicle that nobody uses and convert it into something for your home or business; the big green fire truck begins the conversion into a rolling tool truck.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey looks back at how the show came about, and covers a few of the unique how-to segments. He digs into some of the incredible vehicles, and the ideas and reasoning that helped shape these projects.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey looks back at how the show came about, and covers a few of the unique how-to segments. He digs into some of the incredible vehicles, and the ideas and reasoning that helped shape these projects.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey uses a vinyl wrap to change the color and look of the big green fire truck; restoring accessories and trim without spending much money.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Barn Find: The Richard Petty Story
programme.desc: Stacey delves into the story of the car Richard and Maurice Petty built, its sale and how it disappeared for decades.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: Stacey hits the road to visit one of the members of GEARZ Nation to see if he can lend a hand on his project.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: Stacey shows you how to tackle the one restoration project that is everybody's dream...the frame off restoration. You'll be surprised how simple a job like this can be if you follow his simple tips and tricks and plan the project out properly.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Sgt. Rock Bumper Fabrication; Mickey Harris
programme.desc: Stacey shows you how to find what you're looking for in the aftermarket and how to fabricate and build your own custom mounts. After that, he digs into the history of the Sgt. Rock truck and how it's connected to "the greatest generation."

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: On the Road Again; Cornwell Dealers
programme.desc: Stacey hits the road to help out a Gearz Nation member with his 56 Corvette project; taking a look at some serious questions that people ask about becoming a tool dealer and if it's really worth it.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey dives into the world of the sleeper, by taking a '73 Plymouth Satellite, and begins transforming it into a street pounding monster under the skin.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey changes the drive train and suspension of a classic muscle car; a new top fuel spark plug is tested in competition.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey installs a Katzkin leather interior into a Jeep Compass, jumps back on to the Street Sweeper project to lay out the interior and the rear-end and finally drop that huge Hemi into place.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: Stacey preps the bed of the NAPA Perfect additions truck. From rolling out Herculiner, installing transfer tank, truck boxes, and even a combination generator/compressor, the big Ford begins its Perfect Additions transformation.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: Stacey tackles the air intake, computer and stock exhaust before addressing a front-end suspension lift kit. He gives the truck extra height and support in the rear, then adds new wheels and tires.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: Stacey leads the hunt for a junkyard Jaguar with a rare engine; pin-striping legend Von Hot Rod comes to the GearZ studio and lays down some lines on a hot rod guitar; Stacey's got a steering column that will keep Falcon owners between the lines.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.sub-title: Evolution of the World's Fastest Cat
programme.desc: The Bill Thomas Cheetah was built to challenge Carroll Shelby's Cobra; hunting for the iconic 1963 Cheetah driven by Budd Clusserath at Daytona.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.sub-title: Puttin' the Heart in the Cat
programme.desc: Stacey and the crew pull the skin off a Cheetah Evolution and work on the vehicle's guts; project overviews of the suspension, steering and brakes.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: The crew visits a budding mechanic with a passion for 4x4s; Stacey installs seats in the Cheetah; selecting gauges; air conditioning unit.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: In the current fad of flipping cars for cash, Stacey takes on the good, bad and ugly of a '66 Biscayne that was bought at auction; from suspension, to brakes, battery cables and lug nuts, this beauty turns out to be more of a beast.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: Celebrating four decades of growth and success for Heartthrob Exhaust; replacing a cracked dashboard with aftermarket parts.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: Stacey returns to the Cheetah project with custom wheels and tires; putting a classic Hilborn fuel injection on a modern LS engine.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.desc: Stacey explores the history of the Jaguar D-Type sports car; loosening tight lug nuts; performance parts.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: New Bed on an Old Ford
programme.desc: The crew hopes that a late-model replacement bed can get an old Ford farm truck out of the pastures and back on the highway.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey visits a father-and-son team who are literally upside down in their restoration of an old C10 pickup; back in the garage, Stacey shows the importance of a compressed air system for tools.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.sub-title: The Nuts & Bolts of Building Sgt. Rock
programme.desc: Garage guru Stacey David prepares military tribute truck Sgt. Rock for final assembly.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.sub-title: Sgt. Rock Interior and Other Details
programme.desc: Stacey reveals how to maintain a consistent theme in a custom build by creating door and kick panels that keep a rugged WWII bomber vibe flowing throughout his vehicle's interior.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: What happens when a loved one passes and leaves behind an unfinished project vehicle; How to know what's sitting in the garage; a new dash going into a vehicle; tips for battery relocation and lining up a vehicle's body panels.

programme.title: Gearz
programme.sub-title: Gearz on the Road, and Beyond the Basic Tools
programme.desc: The crew meets a young gear-head whose love of wrenching started with 4-wheelers and has grown to include four-wheel drive pickups.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: The son of legendary car builder Bill Thomas discusses wrenching, racing and his father's Cobra Killer, the Cheetah.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Old Dog -- New Truck: Redoing Your Truck Interior
programme.desc: Stacey takes on the sights and smells of a 1983 Chevy truck that needs immediate attention to its crusty interior.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Reviving an Old Thiokol IMP Snowcat
programme.desc: Stacey brings in a Thiokol Imp snowcat that requires some attention.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Tackling the formidable task of wiring.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: New A/C for an Old Chevy
programme.desc: Stacey demonstrates how easy it is to install a complete air conditioning system in a classic truck just in time for summer.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Sparks fly when Stacey and the crew visit the Cornwell forges to watch molten steel be expertly transformed into high-quality ratchets.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stacey provides a solution for Cheetah lovers to keeping headers both cool and looking red hot; using a stock tank after converting to fuel injection.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: The crew heads to Memphis, Tenn., to help out a local barbecue pitmaster who is rebuilding a 1972 Chevrolet Nova and wants to swap out an automatic transmission with a manual.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: Cheap Fuel Inj., Snow Cat Bed, TN Elec Guitar
programme.desc: Installing an electronic fuel injection system in a carbureted vehicle.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: A look at Super Lite Cars on the road and track; Stacey recalls an earlier SLC build, and introduces two SLC builders who took a hands on approach to putting their cars together.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Master car builder Stacey David shows off the truck that's had the nation talking for ten years; a look back at the kid with the dream.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: On the Road & Unicure Spray Booths
programme.desc: Stacey hits the road to find a project build, ending up with a 1981 Chevrolet Camaro and some awesome stories from the seller.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.sub-title: The Return of Heavy Metal & Snowcat
programme.desc: Hot rod tow truck Heavy Metal returns to the garage for a facelift; Stacey's friends from the Hot Rod Institute drop by discuss his plans for the paint and fabrication work.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Stunt doubles are brought in when the heat gets turned up and even really good drivers cannot keep up.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Face-lift for a 1981 Chevrolet Camaro.

programme.title: Stacey David's GearZ
programme.desc: Old, crusty junk hits the floor as a build inspired by "The Fall Guy" gets a new look, from repainted trim to the door panels.

programme.title: Stingray Classic Rock
programme.desc: Relive the wonders of classic rock; the sound that revolutionized the world of music from the '60s to the '80s. Features the genre's biggest legends such as Janis Joplin, Peter Frampton, and The Rolling Stones.

programme.title: Stingray Remember the '80s
programme.desc: Relive the decade of perms and shoulder pads! The unmistakable synth pop sound of artists such as Michael Jackson, George Michael, and Whitney Houston, is bound to remind you of all your greatest '80s memories!

programme.title: Stingray Hot Country
programme.desc: Today's country music is on fire, and it keeps getting hotter. Hot Country rounds up your favorite hits of the past decade from the biggest stars: Taylor Swift, Blake Shelton, Lady Antebellum, Carrie Underwood, Keith Urban, and more.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Grilled Squid Salad and Crispy Squid on the Plancha
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern creates a vibrant salad where grilled squid is served with shaved fennel, cherry tomatoes, endive, and a lemon dressing. Then, Andrew shares his country-style squid on the plancha with fresh breadcrumbs, chilies, and a homemade aioli.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Poultry Perfection: Moroccan Spiced Guinea Hen with Charred Vegetables

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: For pig roast perfection, Chef Andrew Zimmern marinates a whole hog.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Boar Ribs and Shrimp and Grits
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern makes a homemade barbecue rub, basting sauce and barbecue sauce for wild boar ribs. Next, chef shares his twist on a classic southern shrimp and grits recipe, adding a side of beautiful wild mushrooms.

programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern breaks down a whole tuna and serves it three ways: grilled, raw, and poached.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern makes two different dishes with red snapper: fried snapper fingers with a spicy chile-orange mayonnaise dipping sauce and a slow-roasted whole fish with an herb salad and charred miso sweet potatoes.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern reimagines the Hawaiian plate lunch with a hand chopped venison burger topped with homemade gravy and a fried egg, jumbo grilled shrimp, a humble macaroni salad and a heaping scoop of white rice.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern showcases quail two ways: classic Southern fried quail with lemon-basil aioli, and as an elevated topping for a traditional open-fire paella, complete with crispy socarrat. He proves this small bird makes an extraordinary dinner.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Elk Tenderloin Tostadas and Grilled Rack of Ribs
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern highlights lean elk, first crafting classic Mexican tostadas with grilled tenderloin, avocado, and homemade salsa macha. Then, he grills a whole rack of elk, serving it with creamy colcannon, tarragon emulsion, and rosemary charcoal.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Chicken Fried Deer and Creole Halibut
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern prepares chicken fried axis deer. Next, Chef Zimmern poaches halibut in tomato roux.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern prepares a traditional Northern Italian-style wild boar ragu. Paired with a hearty bowl of pasta. Next, chef Zimmern blackens beautiful crimson filets of steelhead in a screaming hot cast iron pan.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew butchers a whole pheasant to make yakitori-style skewers and a homemade flatbread, cucumber salad and a mango chutney.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern explores Minnesota's forests for wild mushrooms. He creates a simple grilled mushroom salad, then hand-shapes cavatelli pasta tossed with golden chanterelles, butter, garlic, thyme, and colatura. He shares foraging and cleaning tips.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern champions the often-overlooked mackerel with a main salad of pickled filets, charred tomatoes, grilled frisée, and sweet summer peaches. Then, he grills it whole seasoned with caramelized onions, rutabaga mash and charred grapes.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Cooking Course
programme.desc: Gordon's tackles stress free cooking with recipes including sticky pork ribs, meatballs four ways and an amazing chilli chicken with ginger and coriander that just gets better and better with time.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Cooking Course
programme.desc: Gordon teaches you how to get started in the kitchen by cooking delicious pork chops with sweet and sour peppers, pan-fried scallops with crunchy apple salad and a tip that will help you keep your knives sharp.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Cooking Course
programme.desc: Gordon teaches us to cook some of his ultimate brunch dishes, including his irresistible spicy pancakes, a simple and delicious frittata and a fool-proof cheat's soufflé with 3 cheeses.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Cooking Course
programme.desc: Gordon teaches us how to cook for special occasions, including stuffed lamb with spinach and pine nuts, Vietnamese-style fresh prawn rolls and a sumptuous whole sea bass stuffed with fennel, lemon and capers.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Cooking Course
programme.desc: Gordon makes the ultimate slow cooked dishes including caramelized figs with ricotta, phenomenal slow-cooked beef short ribs and melting beef brisket.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a speedy midweek chicken traybake. He shows us cool ways to upgrade salmon, knocks up a shelf-raid quesadilla toastie, and transforms a batch-cook Bolognese into cheesy bread bowls. All finished off with a super simple banoffee tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.sub-title: Creamy Chicken Pot Pie and Chocolate Tiramisu
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a midweek traybake of roast veg and Camembert. He turns poached chicken into a creamy chicken & chive pie, serves up his easy green chilli salsa under sizzling butterflied prawns, and finishes it all off with decadent chocolate tiramisù.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie shares a simple salmon dish perfect for a midweek meal, and some no-fuss fish cakes made from tins. He cooks a lamb Madras traybake, as well as a no-cook pasta sauce, and crowns it all off with a hot and crispy ice cream parcel!

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.sub-title: Jarred Pepper Pasta and Sheet Pan Brisket
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a speedy jarred-pepper pasta, crispy chicken in baked-bread sauce, and a beef brisket traybake - all prepped in minutes. He makes shelf-raid spiced haricot beans, and rounds it all off with a fast and simple strawberry & balsamic tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.sub-title: Smash Burger and Creamy Chicken with Mushrooms
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a midweek meal of speedy sweet pea chicken, a juicy Smash burger and a chicken and mushroom rice traybake. He batch-cooks his seven-veg sauce, followed by a spicy fish soup. And finishes off with a perfect pudding of tinned fruit granita.

programme.title: All Up In My Grill
programme.sub-title: Deluxe Dogs with Crusted Corn & Potatoes
programme.desc: Chef Dale Talde puts a modern spin on a classic All-American cookout with his loaded hot dogs, spiced smashed potato salad, chorizo & corn nut crusted corn, and bangin' banana pudding pops. Sparklers out for this spread!

programme.title: All Up In My Grill
programme.desc: Chef Dale Talde amps up your chicken dishes by making smoky chicken wings, a whole roasted turmeric ginger chicken with tamarind sauce, and chicken patties grilled in banana leaves with chili lime noodles. A frosty lime rickey finishes it all off!

programme.title: All Up In My Grill
programme.desc: Chef Dale Talde gets his beach vibes going with a grilled shrimp & lobster cocktail, clams with lemon breadcrumbs, a killer grilled king crab with burnt citrus butter, and a chilled mango frozen daiquiri. Don't you dare miss this beach party!

programme.title: All Up In My Grill
programme.desc: Ribs are a lifestyle, and Chef Dale's living it up with smoky beef shorties in tangy red wine glaze, Miami-style pork ribs dripping in bacon-guava BBQ, spicy baked beans, and killer key lime caramel cones. Grab some napkins!

programme.title: All Up In My Grill
programme.desc: Chef Dale Talde creates a tasty menu that kids & parents alike will love! Apple pancakes with coconut brown butter syrup, grilled cheese & tomato soup, cheesy chicken parm, and a sticky s'mores dessert rounds out the full day of deliciousness.

programme.title: All Up In My Grill
programme.desc: Chef Dale Talde is throwin' down a pizza spread like never before with his Chicago-style tavern pie with sausage & onion, a grilled pizza pocket with pepperoni & cheese, chicken wings a la pizzaiola, and a zesty grilled caesar salad to finish it off.

programme.title: All Up In My Grill
programme.desc: Chef Dale Talde pulls out all the stops for this daddy-sized dinner. He grills up prime rib with herb & horseradish crust, lobster & chili sauce American, charred green beans with garlic tahini, and cheddar-stuffed parker house rolls.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern demystifies working with live sea urchin and scallops by creating a showstopping seafood risotto.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Quail Noodle Salad and Antelope Stew
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern creates Vietnamese cold noodle salad and grilled quail. Next, chef makes antelope stew.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Southern Shrimp Skewers and Asian Crispy Rice
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern grills beloved shrimp in two distinct ways. He starts with jumbo shrimp and country ham skewers glazed with sweet-hot pepper jelly. Then, he creates Asian-inspired shrimp, served over crispy battle rice and a complex sambal.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Creamy Clam Chowder and Mussels in Spicy Tomato Sauce
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern demystifies cooking clams, mussels and oysters.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Nashville Hot Pheasant and Mint Basil Lemonade
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern takes a spin on Nashville-style hot fried chicken. Hot and spicy, batter coated, crispy fried pheasant is paired with a spinach salad topped with bacon vinaigrette and ice cold mint basil lemonade.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew prepares a crave-able appetizer of bison tenderloin bites served with tamarind sauce and a classic Hunan peanut sauce. Then chef grills prosciutto-wrapped whole trout stuffed with lemon and herbs served with rice pilaf.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Bison Tomahawk Chop with Italian-style Eggplant and Panzanella
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern shares a simple trick for getting the most out Bison tomahawk chops.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Grilled Turkey Legs, Creamed Greens and Lemon Orzo
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern prepares Grilled Turkey Legs, Creamed Greens and Lemon Orzo.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern prepares the ultimate shore lunch of fried walleye filets, served with a tangy homemade tartar sauce, coleslaw, and crispy fried potatoes. Next, chef grills bone-in elk chops with a simple mustard glaze over hardwood charcoal.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Braised Pigeon with Homemade Herb Gnocchi
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern cooks braised squab with olives and vinegar, a perfect meal for a fall evening.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern butchers a whole rabbit, then braises the meat in a rich and flavorful red chile sauce. He tops it off with a simple blended tomatillo salsa and fresh cilantro. Next, chef Zimmern cooks a comforting farmhouse meal of pheasant.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Goat Curry with Rice Pilaf and Carmelized Onions
programme.desc: Chef Andrew butchers a whole goat, using the leg and backstrap for a braised curry.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern goes primal with two antelope dishes: a Greek-style whole leg roasted over the open fire with a lemony avgolemono sauce and crispy potatoes, and a simple antelope tenderloin carpaccio served with roasted tomatoes and parmesan.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern breaks down a whole rabbit to grill with a spicy, Sichuan sauce and plate with panzanella.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern shares his favorite way to prepare wild turkey breast, pan-fried over an open fire. Served with cheese sauce and jalapeno-bacon wild rice. Then, cooks a simple meal of seared salmon and a side of crispy potatoes and onions.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern starts simmering a classic Italian Bolognese sauce, with ground bison, lots of red wine and tomatoes. Then, he adds marinated bison skirt steak tacos with borracho beans to the tailgate menu.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Grilled Eggplant Caponata Toast and Crispy Squab Katsu
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern creates an elegant, seared pigeon toast crowned with a Sicilian caponata bursting with grilled eggplant, olives, and capers and a crispy pigeon katsu served with a refreshing shaved cabbage salad and punchy ginger-carrot vinaigrette.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Zimmern cooks his version of Peking-style goose. Next, Chef Zimmern cooks a meal of striped bass.

programme.title: AE Apocalypse Earth
programme.desc: A group of refugees from Earth land on an exotic planet, where they must fight ruthless aliens to survive.

programme.title: In The Name Of Ben-Hur
programme.desc: A young gladiator enslaved in the Roman arena in 171 A.D. escapes by chariot towards the freedom of Hadrian's Wall. Fighting centurions and mercenaries along the way, he becomes a folk hero for all those struggling to fight Roman oppression.

programme.title: War Of The Worlds 2: The Next Wave
programme.desc: The aliens return to complete their plan of human domination, launching a second wave of attacks that threaten to destroy what is left of humanity.

programme.title: End Times
programme.desc: In the face of a post-apocalyptic world filled with both living and undead, an aging mercenary must team up with a young, desperate, but determined woman to survive the horrors of their new reality.

programme.title: End Times
programme.desc: In the face of a post-apocalyptic world filled with both living and undead, an aging mercenary must team up with a young, desperate, but determined woman to survive the horrors of their new reality.

programme.title: Heretics
programme.desc: Recovered footage documents the horror of a group of teens who break into the abandoned Simmons house. What begins as a thrill-seeking adventure turns into a nightmare as they discover a dark cult intent on sacrificing them one by one.

programme.title: Z Nation
programme.desc: On the quest for a "natural" cure for the Zombie Virus, our Heroes make their way to the greenhouse where "Batch 47" is being grown. Our Heroes meet "Escorpion," leader of the "Zeros."

programme.title: Alien Origin
programme.desc: Recently discovered footage from a lost military expedition exposes the horrifying truth about the genesis of life on earth.

programme.title: Lord of the Elves
programme.desc: In an ancient age, the peace-loving tribe are enslaved by a race of flesh-eating dragon-riders. The young tribesmen Goben must join forces with their neighbor giants, the humans, to free his people and vanquish their enemies.

programme.title: Lord of the Elves
programme.desc: In an ancient age, the peace-loving tribe are enslaved by a race of flesh-eating dragon-riders. The young tribesmen Goben must join forces with their neighbor giants, the humans, to free his people and vanquish their enemies.

programme.title: Dracula's Curse
programme.desc: The peaceful world where vampires and humans live side by side, explodes into a bloody battle when Countess Bathorly and her family of deadly vampires believe they possess the power of Count Draclula himself.

programme.title: Thor: God of Thunder
programme.desc: As fires rage and poison rains down from the sky, Thor must battle giant wolf and snake gods to save the world from Armageddon.

programme.title: Geo-Disaster
programme.desc: A volcano, an earthquake and a tornado converge on Los Angeles to form an unprecedented cataclysm.

programme.title: Geo-Disaster
programme.desc: A volcano, an earthquake and a tornado converge on Los Angeles to form an unprecedented cataclysm.

programme.title: Zombie Apocalypse
programme.desc: As night falls in a small California town, the dead rise from their graves. The townspeople must fight to survive until sunrise.

For millennia, calendars have added an extra day every four years. In doing so, they violated the ancient Mayan calendar. Now we are in the 13th month of the 13th year of the new millennium, and the few who survive will battle a world of demons.

programme.title: Meteor Moon
programme.desc: When a meteor crashes into the moon and shifts its axis, Earth's gravity pulls the moon into the path of the planet. Now, a group of scientists must figure out how to stop the moon from hitting the earth before it's too late.

programme.title: Z Nation
programme.desc: Season 3 picks up where Season 2 left off. Following the explosion of the Sub, Murphy escapes with Dr. Merch and 10K. Warren, Doc, and Addy, having just jumped into Escorpion's truck, are taken prisoner by a group of Pan Asian soldiers.

programme.title: Z Nation
programme.desc: Season 3 picks up where Season 2 left off. Following the explosion of the Sub, Murphy escapes with Dr. Merch and 10K. Warren, Doc, and Addy, having just jumped into Escorpion's truck, are taken prisoner by a group of Pan Asian soldiers.

programme.title: Monster Mash
programme.desc: Dr. Frankenstein wants to meld the world's strongest monsters into a singular, unstoppable creation. The monsters must work together to stop him before it's too late.

programme.title: Fast and Fierce: Death Race
programme.desc: Jack Tyson is on his way to grabbing the cash prize for an illegal car race from Mexico to California until a woman jumps into his car pleading for help.

programme.title: Z Nation
programme.desc: After a dangerous game of Zombie hide-and-seek, an apocalyptic fairytale, and a close call with an Ender, Addy and Doc convince the family to follow them to Murphy.

programme.title: Freakshow
programme.desc: When a gang of thieves, hiding out in a circus sideshow, decides to steal from the wealthy owner, there are horrific consequences!

programme.title: Freakshow

programme.title: Killers
programme.desc: The deal is simple. Buy the stolen drugs for almost nothing, sell them and make a killing. For Ray and the others, it?s the deal of their lives. For Heather, the rich girl, it?s a ride on the wild side.

programme.title: Monster Mash
programme.desc: Dr. Frankenstein melds Dracula, the Werewolf, and the Invisible Man into a singular, unstoppable creation.

programme.title: Z Nation
programme.desc: Polish, subtitled in english. Based on a true story. A provincial town in Poland. The final day of school. A seemingly normal day in the life of three ordinary elementary school students culminates in shocking and terrifying events.

programme.title: Z Nation
programme.desc: Out to get Cassandra back, her bizarre and dangerous "family" of cannibals catches up with the team and kidnaps Addy, endangering the mission.

programme.title: Blood Lake

programme.title: Mercenaries
programme.desc: When a visiting dignitary is captured and imprisoned while touring a war zone, a team of elite female commandoes is assembled to infiltrate the women's prison and rescue her.

programme.desc: After a dangerous game of Zombie hide-and-seek, an apocalyptic fairytale, and a close call with an Ender, Addy and Doc convince the family to follow them to Murphy.

programme.title: Znation

programme.title: Alien Abduction

programme.title: The Holy Land Revealed
programme.desc: What is meant by "holy land"; the difference between archeology and history; how reliable the Hebrew Bible is as a window into life in ancient Israel; answers to these and other questions; the region's earliest recorded inhabitants, the Canaanites.

programme.title: The Holy Land Revealed
programme.desc: What archeologists uncover about the arrival of the Israelites into Canaan; an ancient Egyptian stele with the earliest reference to Israel; the remains of Jericho's walls; a Philistine temple similar to the one Samson destroys in the book of Judges.

programme.title: The Holy Land Revealed
programme.sub-title: Jerusalem - An Introduction to the City
programme.desc: Surveying the topography and layout of Jerusalem; reviewing biblical accounts of Jerusalem from the arrival of David around 1,000 B.C.E. to the start of the Babylonian exile in 586 B.C.E.; the remains of an Assyrian siege on the city of Lachish.

programme.title: The Holy Land Revealed
programme.sub-title: The Jerusalem of David and Solomon
programme.desc: A first look at the biblical City of David; combing through the remains of a scribe's house behind a city wall; the development of biblical Hebrew script; rare examples of this script in a clay sealing, a piece of pottery and a victory stele.

programme.title: Notorious London: A City Tour
programme.desc: London when it is home to Oscar Wilde at the close of the 19th century; the details of Wilde's trial for indecency; the way London society is structured; how immense wealth and privilege, for some, leaves its imprint on the city and its history.

programme.title: Notorious London: A City Tour
programme.sub-title: St. Paul's Cathedral in Faith, Fire, and Sin
programme.desc: St. Paul's Cathedral has overlooked London from a high point in the city for generations; the history of this church that has been much more than a place of worship; it has survived fire and the ravages of war; episodes from the cathedral's past.

programme.title: Notorious London: A City Tour
programme.sub-title: Getting Blitzed at London's Café de Paris
programme.desc: An evening in March of 1941, when the Cafe de Paris is destroyed in the Blitz; the history of the cafe; how its destruction reflects the horrors of wartime London; the resiliency of the citizens who keep calm and carry on under the threat of war.

programme.title: Notorious London: A City Tour
programme.sub-title: On Carnaby Street during the Swinging '60s
programme.desc: Carnaby Street emerges in the 17th century; a symbol of social change in the 1960s; its history; it becomes a magnet for trendsetters and youth culture in post-war London; why these changes are embraced by some and seen as a threat by others.

programme.title: Lost Worlds of South America
programme.sub-title: South America's Lost Cradle of Civilization
programme.desc: The evidence for South America as one of the true wellsprings of human civilization; the diversity of early South American cultures; what the cultures shared; innovations in agriculture, architecture, handicrafts, social organization and religion.

programme.title: Lost Worlds of South America
programme.desc: The first cities in the Americas, dating back to 3000 B.C.; investigating the means of subsistence of coastal and inland valley cities; evidence of trade in marine and agricultural products; learning about the sites of Kotosh, El Paraiso and Caral.

programme.title: Lost Worlds of South America
programme.desc: The earliest evidence of humans in the Americas; Monte Verde and its view into ancient life.

programme.title: Lost Worlds of South America
programme.desc: Observing pivotal changes in northern Peruvian societies; architectural styles; a state identity.

programme.title: Cities of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Cities are integral to modern lives; why wandering ancient humans leave the forests and plains to create settlements; the fundamental question of "why" is the first step toward understanding the inhabitants and lessons from ancient cities.

programme.title: Cities of the Ancient World
programme.sub-title: Çatalhöyük - First Experiment in Urban Living
programme.desc: Imagining a city with no streets, public buildings or common spaces; the world's first city offers an intriguing window into life in the Neolithic era; exploring the remains of Catalhoyuk's family housing, murals and religious shrines.

programme.title: Cities of the Ancient World
programme.desc: The story of the walls of Jericho in the Book of Joshua; looking past the biblical story; what architectural remains suggest about Jericho, whose ritual spaces help create a community and whose walls help define the urban environment.

programme.title: Cities of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Looking at one of the most marvelous cities in the ancient world; Uruk exhibits many of the hallmarks of ancient civilization; division of labor among its craftsmen; a class hierarchy that includes professional priests; records of art and literature.

programme.desc: What is meant by "holy land"; the difference between archeology and history; how reliable the Hebrew Bible is as a window into life in ancient Israel; answers to these and other questions; the region's earliest recorded inhabitants, the Canaanites.

programme.title: The Holy Land Revealed
programme.desc: What archeologists uncover about the arrival of the Israelites into Canaan; an ancient Egyptian stele with the earliest reference to Israel; the remains of Jericho's walls; a Philistine temple similar to the one Samson destroys in the book of Judges.

programme.title: The Holy Land Revealed
programme.sub-title: Jerusalem - An Introduction to the City
programme.desc: Surveying the topography and layout of Jerusalem; reviewing biblical accounts of Jerusalem from the arrival of David around 1,000 B.C.E. to the start of the Babylonian exile in 586 B.C.E.; the remains of an Assyrian siege on the city of Lachish.

programme.title: The Holy Land Revealed
programme.sub-title: The Jerusalem of David and Solomon
programme.desc: A first look at the biblical City of David; combing through the remains of a scribe's house behind a city wall; the development of biblical Hebrew script; rare examples of this script in a clay sealing, a piece of pottery and a victory stele.

programme.title: Notorious London: A City Tour
programme.desc: London when it is home to Oscar Wilde at the close of the 19th century; the details of Wilde's trial for indecency; the way London society is structured; how immense wealth and privilege, for some, leaves its imprint on the city and its history.

programme.title: Notorious London: A City Tour
programme.sub-title: St. Paul's Cathedral in Faith, Fire, and Sin
programme.desc: St. Paul's Cathedral has overlooked London from a high point in the city for generations; the history of this church that has been much more than a place of worship; it has survived fire and the ravages of war; episodes from the cathedral's past.

programme.title: Notorious London: A City Tour
programme.sub-title: Getting Blitzed at London's Café de Paris
programme.desc: An evening in March of 1941, when the Cafe de Paris is destroyed in the Blitz; the history of the cafe; how its destruction reflects the horrors of wartime London; the resiliency of the citizens who keep calm and carry on under the threat of war.

programme.title: Notorious London: A City Tour
programme.sub-title: On Carnaby Street during the Swinging '60s
programme.desc: Carnaby Street emerges in the 17th century; a symbol of social change in the 1960s; its history; it becomes a magnet for trendsetters and youth culture in post-war London; why these changes are embraced by some and seen as a threat by others.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: A Selective History of Infamy
programme.sub-title: A Murder That Almost Killed Hollywood
programme.desc: Unsolved murders are as compelling as anything one might see in movies; the first "crime of the century" is the mysterious murder of a Hollywood director in 1922; William Desmond Taylor is not who he seems to be, and his death continues to fascinate.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: A Selective History of Infamy
programme.sub-title: The Deadly Irish Fairy Tale of 1895
programme.desc: The murder of Bridget Cleary in turn-of-the-century Ireland shows the mystery of human psychology; on the surface, it seems her husband, Michael, murders her because he believes she is a "changeling" fairy in disguise; investigating this motive.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: A Selective History of Infamy
programme.desc: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand is one of the most world-shaking events in the 20th century; the motivation behind the murder is rife with conspiracy; diving into a cycle of mystery and violence in early 20th-century Sarajevo.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: A Selective History of Infamy
programme.sub-title: The Plot to Assassinate the Romanovs
programme.desc: Exploring the murder of the Russian Tsar Nicholas and other Romanovs at the hands of the Bolsheviks; for more than a century, the facts of this murder are dissected and debated; time adds few facts but plenty of speculation and myth to this event.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: A Selective History of Infamy
programme.sub-title: Three Serial Killers of Weimar Germany
programme.desc: Weimar Republic Germany is a psychologically traumatized era of madmen, sex fiends and serial killers; surveying the three Weimar-era murder cases of the sadistic Peter Kurten, the "butcher" Fritz Haarmann and the unsolved Hinterkaifeck Murders.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: A Selective History of Infamy
programme.desc: Delving into one of the best-known and most debated murder cases in American history; Richard Hauptmann is executed for kidnapping and killing aviator Charles Lindbergh's baby; investigating whether he acts alone and who gets the ransom money.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: A Selective History of Infamy
programme.sub-title: Murderous Maids: The Papin Sisters of France
programme.desc: A well-to-do French woman and her daughter are murdered at the hands of two maids who are discovered cowering in the attic and covered in blood; investigating what drives the Papin sisters to kill, and to kill so savagely.

programme.title: Crimes of the Century: A Selective History of Infamy
programme.sub-title: The Moscow-to-Mexico Mission to Kill Trotsky
programme.desc: Political assassinations are rife with conspiracy and often leave a question mark in the history books; examining the murder of Leon Trotsky, an exiled Soviet leader; what appears to be a seamless plot to kill a political rival owes much to chance.

programme.title: Secrets of the Occult
programme.desc: The occult, in the popular imagination, is often associated with evil, cheap magic tricks and superstitions; the world of the occult is much deeper and broader than that, encompassing an entire philosophy and way of life for some believers.

programme.title: Secrets of the Occult
programme.desc: Occultism posits that there we are surrounded by a hidden world inhabited by intelligent beings that we don't typically encounter in our everyday lives.

programme.title: Secrets of the Occult
programme.sub-title: The Christmas Tree and Other Rituals
programme.desc: A look at the occult concepts and symbolism embedded in common religious practices across different traditions, including the Christmas tree and Santa Claus, a Native American Ghost Dance of the 19th century American West and more.

programme.title: Secrets of the Occult
programme.sub-title: Practical Magic: Love, Money, and Health
programme.desc: A look at if occult techniques are just mental hacks that allow people to tap into their natural abilities; turning to the utilitarian side of occultism: practical magic.

programme.title: Secrets of the Occult
programme.desc: A look at the three pillars of occultism: alchemy, astrology and ritual magic.

programme.title: Secrets of the Occult
programme.desc: Delving into the world of spirit communication and necromancy, from Stone Age shamans to Harry Houdini's anti-spiritualist crusade of the early 20th century.

programme.title: Secrets of the Occult
programme.desc: From the late Ice Age to the 14th century, an exploration of the origins and influence of occult ideas and practices.

programme.title: Decoding the Secrets of Egyptian Hieroglyphs
programme.desc: Understanding why ancient Egypt has to invent some form of writing; central to this introduction is a study of the Narmer Palette; its writing sets standards and conventions that would be followed for 3,000 years.

programme.title: Decoding the Secrets of Egyptian Hieroglyphs
programme.desc: Learning the hieroglyphic alphabet; how to write and position each hieroglyph, including a stylized hand, a horned viper and two hieroglyphs for which English does not have a letter; transcribing one's name from English to ancient Egyptian.

programme.title: The American West: History, Myth, and Legacy
programme.desc: Some of the ways people think about the American West; how this vast, fascinating region comes into being; how it is shaped by centuries of myth-making; what about westward expansion fascinates every generation of Americans.

programme.title: The American West: History, Myth, and Legacy
programme.sub-title: The West in the Colonial Era
programme.desc: Understanding the mark left on the American West by its earliest colonists; the Spaniards, who introduce horses; the French, who develop a complex trade system; the English, who have little interest in colonizing the west of the Appalachians.

programme.desc: Doc Holliday retires to Colorado after a long battle with tuberculosis and finds himself in one final adventure to save a young man from ruthless outlaws.

programme.desc: After the Civil War, ex-Confederates en route to Mexico clash with Union horse traders, but they must unite to battle Mexican bandits and revolutionaries threatening them both.

programme.title: Doc Holliday 2
programme.desc: After the OK Coral shootout in Tombstone, Doc Holliday opens a small Faro Saloon in a remote town where he encounters Edward Bailey. When a Faro game goes wrong for Edward, he turns up missing and Doc Holliday finds himself on trial for his murder.
